419	/-- **Type-level Subfunctor presentation of the carrier** (iter-189 Subfunctor
420	restructure). The carrier of `𝒪_C(P)` viewed as a `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor` of
421	the Type-valued presheaf `TopCat.presheafToType C.left.toTopCat C.left.functionField`
422	(the presheaf of arbitrary functions to the function field `K(C)`). A section over
423	an open `U` is a function `g : U.unop → K(C)` that is constant with value
424	`f ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf U`.
425	
426	This Subfunctor packages the carrier conditions uniformly:
427	- At `U ≠ ⊥`: a section is a constant function valued in
428	  `carrierSubmoduleSheaf U = carrierSubmodule U` (so the order conditions hold).
429	- At `U = ⊥`: the unique empty function (witnessed by `f = 0 ∈ ⊥`).
430	
431	Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff`, applied against the ambient
432	sheaf `TopCat.Presheaf.toType_isSheaf` for `presheafToType`, reduces the sheaf
433	condition for the (type-valued shadow of the) carrier to a stalk-locality check:
434	every section of `presheafToType K(C)` whose sieve-of-section is covering already
435	lies in the Subfunctor. On the irreducible scheme `C.left`, this stalk-locality
436	holds because any two non-empty opens intersect, forcing constant-function gluing
437	to agree on overlaps, and the per-prime-divisor order conditions transfer from
438	each open in the cover to their union pointwise. -/

440	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
441	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
442	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
443	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
444	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
445	    CategoryTheory.Subfunctor
446	      (TopCat.presheafToType C.left.toTopCat C.left.functionField) where
447	  obj U := { g : U.unop → C.left.functionField |
448	    ∃ f : C.left.functionField,
449	      f ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh U ∧
450	        g = fun _ => f }
451	  map := by
452	    classical
453	    intro U V i g hg
454	    obtain ⟨f, hf, hgf⟩ := hg
455	    by_cases hV : V.unop = (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
456	    · -- `V = ⊥`: `V.unop` is the empty type. Any function with empty domain works;
457	      -- use the witness `f' = 0 ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf ⊥ = ⊥`.
458	      refine ⟨0, ?_, ?_⟩
459	      · exact (lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh V).zero_mem

888	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
889	
890	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
891	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
892	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
893	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
894	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/

896	/-- **Forward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)).**
897	
898	Given `f ∈ K(C)^×` with `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P`
899	and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`, the rational function `f` lifts to a global section
900	`s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical inclusion
901	`𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`. Hartshorne's construction inside the
902	proof of Proposition 7.7(b) (p. 157) defines this section locally on the
903	affine cover witnessing the Cartier-divisor structure of `[P]`: on a
904	neighbourhood `U_i` of `P` the section is `f · f_P` (in
905	`𝒪_C(P)(U_i) = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_C(U_i)`, this is `(f · f_P) · f_P⁻¹ = f`);
906	on the complement `C ∖ {P}` the section is `f` directly (the order
907	conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` guarantee `f ∈ 𝒪_C(U) = 𝒪_C(P)(U)`

1059	pole at `P`** (Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7, p. 157).

61	/--
62	The functor which maps an object of `D` to the constant sheaf at that object, i.e. the
63	sheafification of the constant presheaf.
64	-/
65	noncomputable def constantSheaf : D ⥤ Sheaf J D := Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ (presheafToSheaf J D)

67	/-- The constant sheaf functor is left adjoint to evaluation at a terminal object. -/

77	/--
78	A sheaf is constant if it is in the essential image of the constant sheaf functor.

80	class IsConstant (F : Sheaf J D) : Prop where
81	  mem_essImage : (constantSheaf J D).essImage F

83	lemma mem_essImage_of_isConstant (F : Sheaf J D) [IsConstant J F] :
84	    (constantSheaf J D).essImage F :=
85	  IsConstant.mem_essImage
86	
87	lemma isConstant_congr {F G : Sheaf J D} (i : F ≅ G) [IsConstant J F] : IsConstant J G where
88	  mem_essImage := essImage.ofIso i F.mem_essImage_of_isConstant
89	
90	lemma isConstant_of_iso {F : Sheaf J D} {X : D} (i : F ≅ (constantSheaf J D).obj X) :
91	    IsConstant J F := ⟨_, ⟨i.symm⟩⟩

94	    (adj : L ⊣ (sheafSections J D).obj ⟨T⟩)
95	    (F : Sheaf J D) : IsConstant J F ↔ L.essImage F := by
96	  rw [essImage_eq_of_natIso (adj.leftAdjointUniq (constantSheafAdj J D hT))]
97	  exact ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩

10	/-!
11	# Adjunctions between additive functors.
12	
13	This provides some results and constructions for adjunctions between functors on
14	preadditive categories:
15	* If one of the adjoint functors is additive, so is the other.
16	* If one of the adjoint functors is additive, the equivalence `Adjunction.homEquiv` lifts to
17	  an additive equivalence `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`.
18	* We also give a version of this additive equivalence as an isomorphism of `preadditiveYoneda`
19	  functors (analogous to `Adjunction.compYonedaIso`), in `Adjunction.compPreadditiveYonedaIso`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe u₁ u₂ v₁ v₂
26	
27	namespace CategoryTheory
28	
29	namespace Adjunction
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Category Functor
32	
33	variable {C : Type u₁} {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₁} C] [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive C]
34	  [Preadditive D] {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj : F ⊣ G)
35	
36	include adj
37	
38	lemma right_adjoint_additive [F.Additive] : G.Additive where
39	  map_add {X Y} f g := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by simp [homEquiv_counit])
40	
41	lemma left_adjoint_additive [G.Additive] : F.Additive where
42	  map_add {X Y} f g := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by simp [homEquiv_unit])
43	
44	variable [F.Additive]
45	
46	/-- If we have an adjunction `adj : F ⊣ G` of functors between preadditive categories,
47	and if `F` is additive, then the hom set equivalence upgrades to an `AddEquiv`.
48	Note that `F` is additive if and only if `G` is, by `Adjunction.right_adjoint_additive` and
49	`Adjunction.left_adjoint_additive`.
50	-/
51	def homAddEquiv (X : C) (Y : D) : AddEquiv (F.obj X ⟶ Y) (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
52	  { adj.homEquiv _ _ with
53	    map_add' _ _ := by
54	      have := adj.right_adjoint_additive
55	      simp [homEquiv_apply] }
56	
57	@[simp]
58	lemma homAddEquiv_apply (X : C) (Y : D) (f : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
59	    adj.homAddEquiv X Y f = adj.homEquiv X Y f := rfl
60	
61	@[simp]
62	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_apply (X : C) (Y : D) (f : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
63	    (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm f = (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f := rfl
64	
65	@[simp]
66	lemma homAddEquiv_zero (X : C) (Y : D) : adj.homEquiv X Y 0 = 0 := map_zero (adj.homAddEquiv X Y)
67	
68	@[simp]
69	lemma homAddEquiv_add (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
70	    adj.homEquiv X Y (f + f') = adj.homEquiv X Y f + adj.homEquiv X Y f' :=
71	  map_add (adj.homAddEquiv X Y) _ _
72	
73	@[simp]
74	lemma homAddEquiv_sub (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
75	    adj.homEquiv X Y (f - f') = adj.homEquiv X Y f - adj.homEquiv X Y f' :=
76	  map_sub (adj.homAddEquiv X Y) _ _
77	
78	@[simp]
79	lemma homAddEquiv_neg (X : C) (Y : D) (f : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
80	    adj.homEquiv X Y (-f) = - adj.homEquiv X Y f := map_neg (adj.homAddEquiv X Y) _
81	
82	@[simp]
83	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_zero (X : C) (Y : D) :
84	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm 0 = 0 := map_zero (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm
85	
86	@[simp]
87	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_add (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
88	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm (f + f') = (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f + (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f' :=
89	  map_add (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm _ _
90	
91	@[simp]
92	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_sub (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
93	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm (f - f') = (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f - (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f' :=
94	  map_sub (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm _ _
95	
96	@[simp]
97	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_neg (X : C) (Y : D) (f : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
98	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm (-f) = - (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f :=
99	  map_neg (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm _
100	
101	open Opposite in
102	/-- If we have an adjunction `adj : F ⊣ G` of functors between preadditive categories,
103	and if `F` is additive, then the hom set equivalence upgrades to an isomorphism between
104	`G ⋙ preadditiveYoneda` and `preadditiveYoneda ⋙ F`, once we throw in the necessary
105	universe lifting functors.
106	Note that `F` is additive if and only if `G` is, by `Adjunction.right_adjoint_additive` and
107	`Adjunction.left_adjoint_additive`.
108	-/
109	def compPreadditiveYonedaIso :
110	    G ⋙ preadditiveYoneda ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{max v₁ v₂} ≅
111	      preadditiveYoneda ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op ⋙
112	        (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{max v₁ v₂} :=
113	  NatIso.ofComponents
114	    (fun Y ↦ NatIso.ofComponents
115	      (fun X ↦ (AddEquiv.ulift.trans ((adj.homAddEquiv (unop X) Y).symm.trans
116	        AddEquiv.ulift.symm)).toAddCommGrpIso)
117	      (fun g ↦ by
118	        ext ⟨y⟩
119	        exact AddEquiv.ulift.injective (adj.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm g.unop y)))
120	    (fun f ↦ by
121	      ext _ ⟨x⟩
122	      exact AddEquiv.ulift.injective ((adj.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm x f)))
123	
124	lemma compPreadditiveYonedaIso_hom_app_app_apply (X : Cᵒᵖ) (Y : D)
125	    (a : ULift.{max v₁ v₂, v₁} (Opposite.unop X ⟶ G.obj Y)) :
126	      ((adj.compPreadditiveYonedaIso.hom.app Y).app X) a =
127	        ULift.up ((adj.homEquiv (Opposite.unop X) Y).symm (AddEquiv.ulift a)) := rfl
128	
129	lemma compPreadditiveYonedaIso_inv_app_app_apply (X : Cᵒᵖ) (Y : D)
130	    (a : ULift.{max v₁ v₂, v₂} (F.obj (Opposite.unop X) ⟶ Y)) :
131	      ((adj.compPreadditiveYonedaIso.inv.app Y).app X) a =
132	        ULift.up ((adj.homEquiv (Opposite.unop X) Y) (AddEquiv.ulift a)) := rfl
133	
134	end Adjunction
135	
136	end CategoryTheory

For (c), the blueprint route is:
- Assume `principal f hf_ne = 0`.
- Then `Scheme.WeilDivisor.order_principal Q f` is zero everywhere: ord_Q(f) = 0 for all primes Q.
- So `f` is a unit at every stalk.
- Over alg-closed `kbar`, on a complete geom-irred integral curve, `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = kbar` (Hartshorne I.3.4, Stacks 01XU).
- So `f ∈ kbar`, say `f = algebraMap kbar K(C) c` for some `c`.
- Then `f = c • 1 = c • toFunctionField s₁ = toFunctionField (c • s₁)` (using `htF_smul` and `hs₁`).
- But `toFunctionField s = f = toFunctionField (c • s₁)`.
- By injectivity, `s = c • s₁ ∈ span {s₁}`, contradicting `hs_not_const`.

If Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4 is not directly available in Mathlib, the (c) close requires a new project-local helper. SEARCH MATHLIB FIRST:

- `lean_local_search` for "functionField" + "constant" + "alg-closed" + "proper" combinations
- `lean_leansearch` for "global sections of structure sheaf on complete integral variety over algebraically closed field"
- Try names like `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField_eq_const_of_isProper`, `Scheme.global_sections_const_of_proper_geom_integral`, etc.

If unavailable: leave (c) as a typed `sorry` with the contradiction setup spelled out as far as possible:
```
  intro hprinc
  -- principal f hf_ne = 0 ⟹ ord_Q(f) = 0 for all prime divisors Q
  -- (Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4): on a complete geom-irred curve over alg-closed kbar,
  -- f ∈ Γ(C, 𝒪_C)^× = kbar^×. So f = algebraMap kbar K(C) c for some c.
  -- Then s = c • s₁ via toFunctionField_injective + htF_smul + hs₁, contradicting hs_not_const.
  -- INFRASTRUCTURE: requires Scheme.functionField_const_of_complete_curve (Stacks 02P0).
  sorry
```

But before giving up on (c), check informal agent (env: `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` etc.) — call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with this exact prompt: "On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve C over an algebraically closed field kbar, with C integral, prove that any rational function f : K(C) whose principal Weil divisor `principal f hf` is zero must satisfy `f ∈ algebraMap kbar K(C)`-image, i.e. is a constant. Use only current Mathlib API. Give a Lean 4 proof body."

## Constraints

- File is `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean` ONLY. Do not edit other .lean files.
- DO NOT touch the sorries at L1147 (`h1_vanishing_genusZero`) and L1209 (`h0_sub_h1_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_eq_two`).
- Project uses `lean4` plugin: use `lean_diagnostic_messages`, `lean_goal`, `lean_multi_attempt`, `lean_local_search`, `lean_leansearch` etc.
- Build via `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.OCofP` (takes ~14s).
- Zero axioms — no `axiom`, no `Classical.choice` wrappers that aren't already in scope, no `decide`-on-Prop.
- If a step keeps failing after 3-4 attempts, leave a partial proof body with comments and progress to the next.

## Reporting

Write to `.archon/task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_RiemannRoch_OCofP.lean.md` with:
- Summary of sorry count before → after
- For (a), (b), (c): RESOLVED / PARTIAL / DEFERRED with specific reasons
- Why I stopped (real progress / partial progress / infrastructure missing)

You have full autonomy. Push HARDER per project standing user hint; don't be conservative with attempts. Decompose, use helpers, persistent multi_attempt probing for the right rewrites. Good luck.

10	/-!
11	# The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point and its global sections (RR.3)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.3** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)` and the
22	  degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and the Riemann–Roch dimension formula
25	  `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0`.
26	- **`RR.3` (this file)**: the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point
27	  `P ∈ C`, its `k̄`-vector space of global sections as the
28	  Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`, the `H¹`-vanishing
29	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a genus-`0` curve, the dimension formula
30	  `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`, and the existence of a non-constant
31	  rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with at most a simple pole at `P`.
32	- `RR.4` (`RationalCurveIso.lean`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
34	
35	## Status (iter-183 Lane A — sig amend + carrierSet scaffold)
36	
37	Iter-183 Lane A (re-dispatch from iter-182 deferral) landed:

41	   so the subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier can read off the order at `P` via the

43	   for the Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` direct construction (per analogist

56	The 5 pinned declarations are:
57	
58	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint` — the invertible
59	   sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` associated to a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
60	   curve `C / k̄`.
61	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
62	   — the identification of global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` with the
63	   Riemann–Roch space
64	   `L([P]) = {f ∈ K(C)^× | div(f) + [P] ≥ 0} ∪ {0}`,
65	   expressed as an `Iff`-style characterisation of the order conditions
66	   `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
67	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.h1_vanishing_genusZero`
68	   — the cohomological vanishing `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a smooth proper
69	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, via the long
70	   exact sequence of the closed-point short exact sequence
71	   `0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`.
72	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
73	   — the dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus `0`,
74	   specialising the χ-identity `RR.2` to `D = [P]` and consuming the
75	   `H¹`-vanishing of pin 3.
76	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
77	   — the corollary: a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` regular
78	   on `C \ {P}` with at most a simple pole at `P`, obtained as a lift of
79	   any non-zero element of the quotient
80	   `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) / 𝓀̄ · 1`.

1294	2. **Function-field extraction.** Set `f := toFunctionField P hP hPcoh s`,

1299	   to read off the pair `(∀ Q ≠ P, 0 ≤ ord_Q f) ∧ (-1 ≤ ord_P f)`.

1456	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
1457	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`

112	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
113	
114	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
115	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
116	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
117	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
118	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
119	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
120	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
121	
122	namespace Scheme
123	
124	/-! ### Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier (iter-183 Lane A scaffold)
125	
126	The substantive iter-183 contribution: a concrete per-open `Set`-valued
127	carrier of the line bundle `𝒪_C(P)`, realised directly as Hartshorne's
128	subsheaf of the function-field constant sheaf `K_C` (Hartshorne II §6
129	p. 144; analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
130	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
131	
132	A section of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U` is a rational function `f ∈ K(C)`
133	satisfying the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
134	`Q ≠ P` with `Q.point ∈ U` (regularity on the complement of `P` inside
135	`U`) and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
136	The construction is independent of the choice of uniformiser at `P`:
137	any two uniformisers differ by a unit, so the order-`≥ −1` condition at
138	`P` is intrinsic.
139	
140	iter-184+ will upgrade `carrierSet` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the
141	closure proofs (zero / addition / kbar-scalar), bundle the result as a
142	presheaf functor (identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, monotone in `U` via
143	`carrierSet_mono`), and discharge the sheaf property via gluing-by-
144	stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at each prime divisor). -/
145	
146	/-- **Carrier set** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U : (Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`: the
147	set of rational functions `f ∈ K(C)` satisfying the order conditions
148	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q` with `Q.point ∈ U.unop`,
149	`Q.point ≠ P`, and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U.unop`.
150	
151	iter-183 Lane A landed this as a concrete substantive `Set` definition;
152	no sorry. -/
153	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet
154	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
155	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
156	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
157	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
158	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
159	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
160	    Set C.left.functionField := by
161	  let Phat : C.left.PrimeDivisor := ⟨P, hPcoh⟩
162	  haveI := Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
163	    (X := C.left) Phat
164	  exact { f | (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ∈ U.unop.1 → Q.point ≠ P →
165	              0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
166	              (P ∈ U.unop.1 → (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Phat f) }
167	
168	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSet` in `U`**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` (i.e.
169	the open `V` is contained in the open `U`), the carrier on `U` is
170	INCLUDED in the carrier on `V` (the order conditions over the smaller
171	open `V` involve fewer prime divisors, hence are easier to satisfy).
172	
173	This is the substantive monotonicity that drives the (identity-on-`K(C)`)
174	restriction map of the would-be `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf` functor:
175	in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`, an arrow `U ⟶ V` corresponds to `V.unop ⊆ U.unop`,
176	and the restriction map `carrierSet U → carrierSet V` is the inclusion
177	delivered by this lemma.
178	
179	iter-183 Lane A landed this as the substantive monotonicity proof; no
180	sorry. -/
181	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet_mono
182	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
183	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
184	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
185	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
186	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
187	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ}
188	    (hUV : V.unop.1 ⊆ U.unop.1) :
189	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
190	      ⊆ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh V := by
191	  intro f hf
192	  refine ⟨fun Q hQV hQP => hf.1 Q (hUV hQV) hQP, fun hPV => hf.2 (hUV hPV)⟩
193	
194	/-- **Nonempty top-open**: for an integral scheme `X`, the top open is
195	nonempty (its carrier is `Set.univ` and `X` itself is nonempty since
196	`IsIntegral X ⟹ IrreducibleSpace X ⟹ Nonempty X`). Supplied as an
197	instance so that the standard Mathlib `Algebra Γ(X, U) X.functionField`
198	instance fires at `U = ⊤`. -/
199	instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instNonemptyTopOpen
200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] :
201	    Nonempty ((⊤ : X.Opens) : Scheme) :=
202	  (Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mpr
203	    ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty X).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
204	
205	/-- **`kbar`-algebra structure on the function field `K(C)`**. The
206	project's `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraSection` instance gives
207	`Algebra kbar Γ(C.left, ⊤)`, and Mathlib's standard
208	`AlgebraicGeometry.instAlgebraCarrierObjOppositeOpens...` gives
209	`Algebra Γ(C.left, ⊤) K(C)` via `germToFunctionField`. Composing the
210	two algebra maps via `RingHom.toAlgebra` produces the desired
211	`Algebra kbar K(C)` instance, which is needed for `Submodule kbar K(C)`

213	noncomputable instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField
214	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
215	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsIntegral C.left] :
216	    Algebra kbar C.left.functionField := by
217	  haveI : Nonempty (⊤ : C.left.Opens) :=
218	    (AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff
219	      (X := C.left) (U := (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).mpr
220	      ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty C.left).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
221	  exact RingHom.toAlgebra
222	    ((Scheme.germToFunctionField C.left (⊤ : C.left.Opens)).hom.comp
223	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
224	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom)

431	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
432	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`

437	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities

439	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
440	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
441	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
442	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
443	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
444	  toFun u :=
445	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))

447	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
448	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
449	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
450	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
451	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
452	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
453	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
454	    congr 1
455	    apply Finsupp.ext
456	    intro Y
457	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
458	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
459	    rw [Units.val_one]
460	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
461	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
462	  map_mul' u v := by
463	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
464	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
465	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
466	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
467	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
468	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
469	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)

/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`

The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
input.new_string:
/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`

The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/

/-- **`toFunctionField` is injective on global sections.**

The composition `s ↦ toFunctionField s` factors as the chain
`HModule_zero_linearEquiv → adj.homEquiv → evaluate at (1 : kbar) →
Subtype.val`, each of which is injective. Used internally to
extract non-vanishing of the rational function from non-vanishing of the
underlying section (in particular: `s ∉ span {s₁}` implies `s ≠ 0` implies
`toFunctionField s ≠ 0 = toFunctionField 0`).

iter-196 Lane A second body push: substrate helper for the
`exists_nonconstant_rational_from_dim_eq_two` close (step (a) `f ≠ 0`). -/
private lemma toFunctionField_injective
    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
    Function.Injective
      (Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField (C := C) P hP hPcoh) := by
  set hT : Limits.IsTerminal (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :=
    Preorder.isTerminalTop _
  set F := lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh
  set adj := (CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT)
  intro s t hst
  -- Unfold `toFunctionField` on both sides.
  change ((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map
        ((Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s)).app
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
      ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar))).1 =
    ((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map
        ((Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) t)).app
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
      ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar))).1 at hst
  -- Step 1: peel off `Subtype.val`.
  have hst_val := Subtype.ext_iff.mpr hst
  -- Set short names for the sheaf homs.
  set fs := (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s
  set ft := (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) t
  -- Step 2: rewrite each side via `homEquiv_unit`.
  have h_rewrite : ∀ (sh : (CategoryTheory.constantSheaf
        (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) ⟶ F),
      ((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map sh).app
          (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
        ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar))) =
        ((adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F) sh).hom 1 := by
    intro sh
    have hrew : ((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map sh).app
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) =
        ((CategoryTheory.sheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
          (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj
            (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).map sh := rfl
    rw [hrew]
    have h3 := adj.homEquiv_unit (X := ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) (Y := F) (f := sh)
    rw [h3]
    exact ModuleCat.comp_apply _ _ _
  -- Subtype.val is injective, extracting equality of the underlying elements.
  rw [Subtype.ext_iff] at hst_val  -- already an equality
  -- Actually `hst` (after `Subtype.ext_iff.mpr`) gave us the val equality;
  -- let's just use the original `hst` which IS the val equality.
  have hval_eq : (((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map fs).app
          (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
        ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar)) : _) : _) = _ := by
    exact_mod_cast Subtype.val_injective hst
  -- Equality after `homEquiv_unit` rewrite.
  rw [h_rewrite fs, h_rewrite ft] at hval_eq
  -- Step 3: φ.hom 1 = ψ.hom 1 (as kbar-elements of the underlying type)
  -- implies φ = ψ via LinearMap.ext_ring on the kbar-linear maps.
  set φ_cat := (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F) fs
  set ψ_cat := (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F) ft
  change φ_cat.hom 1 = ψ_cat.hom 1 at hval_eq
  have hφψ : φ_cat = ψ_cat := by
    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
    apply LinearMap.ext_ring
    exact hval_eq
  -- Step 4: peel off `adj.homEquiv`.
  have hfs_ft : fs = ft :=
    (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F).injective hφψ
  -- Step 5: peel off `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` (a LinearEquiv).
  exact (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F).injective hfs_ft

495	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the

499	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas

502	iters of `RR.1`. -/
503	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
504	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]

540	/-! ## §6. Positive part of a Weil divisor
541	
542	Iter-190 plan-phase Lane I Pin 2 corrective substrate. The carrier
543	`X.WeilDivisor = X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ` is a finitely supported integer-valued
544	function on prime divisors; the *positive part* `(D)_0` is obtained by
545	replacing each coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)`. Equivalently this is
546	the lattice join `D ⊔ 0` in the pointwise lattice structure (the
547	`semilatticeSup` instance on `Finsupp` is noncomputable). The complementary
548	*negative part* `(D)_∞ := (-D)_0` then gives the canonical decomposition
549	`D = (D)_0 - (D)_∞` into a difference of effective divisors.
550	
551	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` /
552	`lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
553	`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6 (iter-190 plan-phase additions).
554	-/
555	
556	/-- **Positive part of a Weil divisor**.
557	
558	`positivePart D` is the divisor obtained from `D` by replacing each
559	coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)` (equivalently `D ⊔ 0` in the
560	pointwise lattice structure on `Finsupp`). For `D = div(f)` on a smooth
561	proper curve `C`, this recovers the divisor-of-zeros `(f)_0` — the
562	formal sum of the zeros of `f` with their multiplicities.
563	
564	The complementary `negativePart D := positivePart (-D)` then gives
565	`D = positivePart D - negativePart D` as a difference of effective
566	divisors.
567	
568	Implementation: `Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)`, the
569	generic Mathlib packaging that maps a finsupp's coefficients through a
570	zero-preserving function. (The `D ⊔ 0` lattice form is equivalent but
571	requires the noncomputable `Finsupp.semilatticeSup` synthesis on
572	`PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ`; the explicit `mapRange` form is more transparent
573	to typeclass synthesis.)
574	
575	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` (project-bespoke;
576	chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6, Hartshorne II.6.10 phrasing). -/
577	noncomputable def positivePart (D : X.WeilDivisor) : X.WeilDivisor :=
578	  Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp) D
579	
580	/-- The positive part of the zero divisor is zero. -/
581	@[simp]
582	lemma positivePart_zero : positivePart (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
583	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
584	    (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
585	  exact Finsupp.mapRange_zero
586	
587	/-- **Degree of positive part as a sum of capped coefficients.** A purely
588	symbolic Mathlib manipulation: unfolding `positivePart`
589	(= `Finsupp.mapRange (max · 0)`) and `degree` (= `Finsupp.sum (fun _ n => n)`)

  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := by
    intro hs_zero
    apply hs_not_const
    rw [hs_zero]
    exact (Submodule.span kbar _).zero_mem
  have hf_ne : f ≠ 0 := by
    intro hf_zero
    apply hs_ne

    rw [htF_zero]; exact hf_zero
  -- (b) Order conditions via `globalSections_iff_mpr`.

    ⟨s, hf_def.symm⟩

  -- (c) `principal f hf_ne ≠ 0`: contrapositive route.
  -- If `principal f = 0`, then `ord_Q(f) = 0` for every prime divisor `Q`.

  -- `htF_smul` and `hs₁`. By `toFunctionField_injective`, `s = c • s₁`,
  -- contradicting `hs_not_const`.

      Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f = 0 := by
    intro Q

  -- or equivalent (Stacks 02P0). The contrapositive setup is complete;
  -- once the substrate lemma lands, the rest of the close is mechanical.
  have hf_const : ∃ c : kbar, f = algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c := by
    sorry
  obtain ⟨c, hfc⟩ := hf_const
  -- Step (c.3): `algebraMap kbar K(C) c = c • 1 = c • toFunctionField s₁`.
  have hf_smul_one : f = c • (1 : C.left.functionField) := by
    rw [hfc]; simp [Algebra.smul_def]
  -- Step (c.4): `toFunctionField (c • s₁) = c • toFunctionField s₁ = c • 1 = f`.
  have h_target : Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
      (C := C) P hP hPcoh (c • s₁) = f := by
    rw [htF_smul c s₁, hs₁, ← hf_smul_one]
  -- Step (c.5): apply injectivity to get `s = c • s₁`.
  have hs_eq : s = c • s₁ := by
    apply toFunctionField_injective (C := C) P hP hPcoh
    rw [← hf_def, ← h_target]
  -- Step (c.6): `s = c • s₁ ∈ span {s₁}`, contradicting `hs_not_const`.
  apply hs_not_const
  rw [hs_eq]
  exact Submodule.smul_mem _ c (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self s₁)

Available Mathlib API in this Lean 4 + mathlib project:

Give a concrete Lean 4 proof sketch using only current Mathlib (snapshot ~b80f227). Keep it under 80 lines. If a project-local helper of size ≤30 LOC is needed, state its statement and proof. Don't invoke 'sorry'." 2>&1 | head -200

input.new_string:
-- Step 5: peel off `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` (a LinearEquiv).
  exact (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F).injective hfs_ft

/-- **Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4 substrate helper.**

A nonzero rational function `f : K(C)` on a smooth proper geometrically
irreducible curve `C / k̄` (with `k̄` algebraically closed and `C`
integral) whose order vanishes at every prime divisor is the image of a
constant under `algebraMap kbar K(C)`:
```
(∀ Q : C.PrimeDivisor, ord_Q(f) = 0)  →  ∃ c : kbar, f = algebraMap kbar K(C) c.
```

This is the classical Hartshorne I.3.4 statement (`Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = k̄` on a
complete geom-irreducible variety over an algebraically closed field)
combined with the "no zeros / no poles ⟹ globally regular" form of
Stacks 02P0. **Mathlib gap** as of snapshot `b80f227`: Mathlib does not
ship `Scheme.functionField_const_of_proper_geometricallyIrreducible` or
equivalent. Closure requires two ingredients:

1. **Algebraic Hartogs on a normal Noetherian scheme**: a rational function
   regular at every codim-1 point extends to a global section of the
   structure sheaf (Stacks tag `0BCK`). On a smooth curve `C`, every prime
   divisor is a codim-1 point.
2. **Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = k̄ for proper geom-irreducible curves over alg-closed k̄**
   (Hartshorne I.3.4): a proper geom-irreducible reduced variety over an
   alg-closed field has its global sections of the structure sheaf coinciding
   with the base field, via the finite-dimensionality of Γ over `k̄`
   (cohomological argument, Hartshorne III.5.2) plus algebraic closure
   forcing all elements to be roots of polynomials, hence in `k̄`.

Both ingredients are project-bespoke Mathlib gaps. The current iter leaves
this as a single typed sorry; the named-helper extraction makes the
upstream dependency explicit for future iterations.

`exists_nonconstant_rational_from_dim_eq_two`. -/
private lemma functionField_const_of_complete_curve_of_orderZero
    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
    (_hord : ∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
        Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f = 0) :
    ∃ c : kbar, f = algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c := by
  -- Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4: two-step closure as documented above.
  --
  -- Step (i): "algebraic Hartogs" extension to a global section. The
  -- hypothesis `_hord : ∀ Q, ord_Q(f) = 0` says `f` has no zeros and no
  -- poles at any prime divisor. On a smooth curve `C / k̄`, every codim-1
  -- point is a prime divisor (the `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` instance
  -- ensures the DVR stalks at those points and identifies `ord_Q` with
  -- the standard valuation). Algebraic Hartogs (Stacks 0BCK; on a
  -- normal Noetherian scheme, `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = ⋂_{Q codim 1} 𝒪_{C, Q}`)
  -- then exhibits `f` as a global section of `𝒪_C`.
  --
  -- Step (ii): `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = k̄`. On a proper geom-irreducible reduced
  -- variety over an algebraically closed field, `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) → k̄` (the
  -- structural inclusion of constants) is an isomorphism. Hartshorne
  -- I.3.4: a finite-dim `k̄`-algebra that is connected and reduced is a
  -- product of fields; alg-closedness forces each factor to equal `k̄`;
  -- connectedness forces a single factor; reducedness is automatic for
  -- a `k̄`-algebra over alg-closed `k̄`. The finite-dimensionality of
  -- `Γ(C, 𝒪_C)` over `k̄` is the standard cohomological fact for proper
  -- varieties (Hartshorne III.5.2).
  --
  -- Both steps require Mathlib infrastructure not currently shipped at
  -- snapshot `b80f227`:
  --   * (i) Algebraic Hartogs on a `IsLocallyNoetherian` + normal scheme
  --     with the codim-1 valuation description.
  --   * (ii) `Module.Finite k̄ Γ(C, 𝒪_C)` for `C` proper over `k̄`, plus
  --     the "connected reduced k̄-algebra finite over alg-closed k̄ is k̄"
  --     argument.
  --
  -- Future iterations: when (i) lands as `Scheme.functionField_extend_global`
  -- (or similar) and (ii) lands as
  -- `Scheme.globalSections_eq_field_of_isProper_of_isGeometricallyIrreducible`,
  -- the body becomes a 5-line composition.
  sorry
